content:
- Processing Services Check-in Office Hours – Structured Summary for Ramki Gaddipati’s Digital Twin

- II. Overall Context of the Conversation/Call

- The agenda centered around updates and decisions concerning Zeta’s Processing Services, touching on organizational structure, new team members, ongoing operational incidents, and product/process enhancements. The team presented progress on dispute management, automation in key workflows, and outlined technical blockers/bottlenecks. Several deep dives occurred into the operational effectiveness of Sparrow, HDFC Pixel, and PayZapp, including descriptions of ongoing automation, error tracking, and reconciliation efforts. Throughout, Ramki Gaddipati’s perspectives were emphasized: welcoming new talent, ensuring inter-team knowledge transfer, seeking rigor in structural planning (esp. product operations), driving process-centric improvements, and advising on KPIs and reporting best practices. Action items, particularly around configuration, documentation, cross-team coordination, and the institutionalization of learnings (especially for presales, product, and support teams), were prioritized.

- IV. Key Takeaways from the Call

- Team Restructuring & New Onboarding: The processing services team has brought on new members with domain expertise and restructured responsibilities to ensure better domain coverage and knowledge transfer ("Rohit and Neha join, roles clarified").
- Exception Management Progress: Multiple process improvements and automation initiatives were highlighted, especially in the Sparrow platform, resulting in improved SAT scores and reduced dispute/processing times ("Dispute flow automation impacts KPIs positively").
- Dispute Handling Modernized: Automation in claim/questionnaire flows and negative file management are now live in Sparrow, with additional features to log disputes at authorization stage—rare in industry practice ("Claims/disputes now handled earlier in lifecycle").
- Knowledge & Best Practice Institutionalization: Ramki pressed for systematic capture of unique value-adds and non-traditional features to feed presales and product differentiation ("Confidence repository and whitepapers to be created").
- Reason Code & LOV Unification Mandated: Inconsistencies in reason/master code management were flagged; Ramki instructed the use of a centralized List-Of-Values (LOV), managed via Delta and exposed through APIs, to ensure uniformity across UI/product layers ("List-of-values should be platform resource, not UI only").
- GL Mapping & Automation: Automation work in GL mapping/fix in several areas (including loan cancellation, UPI reversals) progressed, with ongoing issues escalated to respective team members ("GL automation expanded, SRS reconciliation engine resolves exceptions").
- Critical Data Table & E-Statement Automation Dependency: Discussion flagged a risk in relying on specific Redshift tables for e-statement triggers, with replication/refresh concern acknowledged; Ramki asked for deeper cross-team transparency here ("E-statement retrigger depends on data freshness, risk flagged").
- Incident Learnings & Recovery Completion: Incident with PayZapp’s manual processing error resulted in clear learnings: stricter best practices, UAT/production consistency, and mandatory use of OPS Center for all activities. Most financial exposure now recovered.
- Adjustment File and Standardization Gaps in UPI Processing: Adjustment file processing in UPI not yet fully automated; Ramki acknowledged gap and urged a platform-wide, IFSC-centric approach ("Standardization across org codes—must be achieved").
- Expectations on KPIs & Numerical Reporting: Ramki sought better metrics and KPIs to be included in future updates, in addition to qualitative process stories.
- Cross-team Stakeholder Handshake: Product operations and platform support teams should inform each other closely in their design stages to avoid redundancy and ensure handshake of responsibilities ("Include Girish and other stakeholders throughout operations design discussions").

- V. Ramki's Insights

1. Value of New Team Members & Experience: Ramki welcomed new team members and emphasized leveraging their extensive domain knowledge, particularly in UPI and payments reconciliations, to strengthen Zeta’s processing capability. He showed appreciation for individual backgrounds and probed into their past operational success, especially concerning user adoption metrics and volume-handling expertise.
2. Convergence & Handshake Between Teams: Insisted that operations and product operations design be transparent across stakeholders (specifically mentioning Girish), highlighting the importance of inter-team handshakes to cover overlapping concern areas. He stressed that teams should keep each other informed at every stage and avoid uncoordinated redundancies.
3. Institutionalizing Learnings for Presales/Marketing: Ramki advocated for compiling and maintaining a repository (“confidence site”) of platform capabilities that are above-and-beyond legacy systems, to be used in presales materials and whitepapers. He positioned such documentation as essential for credible sales narratives and internal confidence.
4. Standardization & Centralization Vision: Argued that features such as reason codes and other user-facing list values must be configurable centrally (not hardwired in UI layers), managed by resource types in Delta, consumed via APIs, and standardized across all user interfaces, workflows, and org codes. He elaborated that such centralization supports future scalability, easier maintenance, and cross-client consistency.
5. Best Practice Process Design: Stressed the value of cross-team involvement from design through UAT to production in critical workflows, especially for incident-prone or customer-facing processes. He underscored that engineering, QA, product, and operational teams all need best-practices guides and systemic education, not just process documentation.
6. KPI-driven Operations: Ramki concluded that while qualitative operational updates are important, equally important is tracking and reporting numerical KPIs—he expects future updates to balance stories with hard data to allow business process benchmarking and strategic decision making.

- VI. Ramki's Suggested Action Items

1. Maintain and Update Confidence Repository
- Task: Continuously compile and document features, value-adds, and process improvements that differentiate Zeta’s platforms from traditional/legacy systems.
- Assigned To: Umesh Bane (main), with team contributions (including Shashank Pilgaonkar).
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Structure repository as a “confidence site” or whitepaper-ready document; ensure customer stories and technical differentiators are accessible and regularly updated; highlight features rarely/never seen elsewhere.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Ongoing; bring notable updates to presales and office hours as they arise.

2. Ensure Centralization of Reason Codes/LOV via Delta Resource
- Task: Move all reason codes, master codes, and similar lists to a centralized, API-exposed resource (List-of-Values) managed in Delta, ensuring front-end systems consume via API not hardcoded lists.
- Assigned To: Abhishek Bhardwaj (drive), Ashish Patel (include in OPS product backlog), Delta/Platform team.
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: All user interfaces and backend processes referencing enumerations/lists should consume from a standard, reusable resource type; avoid UI-level code changes for list updates, and document the resource type for future workflows.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Add to product backlog immediately; as soon as possible—no explicit hard deadline given.

3. Cross-functional Handshake in Operations Design
- Task: Proactively involve Girish and all relevant stakeholders in product operations and platform support services design, ensuring responsibilities, closures, and overlaps are well delineated.
- Assigned To: Uttam Rao (coordination), Girish (to be looped in).
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Keep all teams informed at all stages of functional design; clearly document boundaries, redundancies, and handshake points; share drafts and seek periodic feedback.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Immediate; ensure this is factored “every step of the way.”

4. Incident Documentation and Process Improvement Institutionalization
- Task: For any operational incident with process implications (e.g., PayZapp manual processing error), formalize root-cause documentation and integrate learnings into SOPs and best practice guides.
- Assigned To: Kizhakkekara Pothiyath Gopakumar (lead), with Amrit, Uttam Rao, and all OPS Center leads.
- Ramki's Instructions: Each major incident should result in documented best practice guidance, with focus on UAT/production parity, and a clear protocol on OPS Center execution; use these as templates for similar future scenarios and for educating engineering and QA teams.
- Timeline: Immediate; after each incident and as learnings arise.

5. E-Statement Data Table Risk Escalation
- Task: Escalate the risk around unrefreshed/unreliable tables (esp. in Redshift) for e-statement automation to the right technical forums; ensure any outage/replication issue is broadly communicated and tracked.
- Assigned To: Navkanth Kanapala (drive technical solution), Umesh Bane (operations oversight), supported by Chandra/Rahul Dibrival (platform).
- Ramki's Instructions: Document potential risks, formalize notification protocol for all downstream teams, and bring unresolved technical blockers to office hours so leadership can intervene if needed.
- Timeline: Ongoing; as issues surface.

6. UPI Adjustment File Processing Platformization
- Task: Enhance UPI processing flows to accept and process adjustment files from NPCI automatically; standardize so all future org codes/lines of business have similar capability.
- Assigned To: PayZapp operations/UPI Product teams (Nikhil, Gopakumar), with oversight from Ramki/Uttam.
- Ramki's Instructions: Treat this as a platform-level requirement, build with IFSC and org code extensibility; prioritize automation and reconciliation to eliminate manual interventions.
- Timeline: No explicit deadline; prioritize as next in list of enhancements.

- VII. Jira Ticket

1. Scope: Transition Reason Codes/Value Lists to Centralized Delta Resources
- Assignee: Abhishek Bhardwaj (Delta/platform team), Ashish Patel (OPS product backlog owner)
- Justification: Technical/operational debt has accumulated from scattering list-of-values management across UI/backend. Ramki identified this as a platform-wide, persistent friction and directed standardization. Impact is cross-functional; will support scalability and reduce error/inconsistency.
> Action items around documentation, confidence repositories, knowledge transfer, incident learnings, e-statement risk management, and cross-team handshakes are broader process and SOP improvements, not suitable for Jira tracking as implementation tickets at this stage; they are ongoing management/organizational tasks.

2. Scope: UPI Adjustment File Processing System Enhancement
- Assignee: Nikhil (UPI Product Lead) with support from Gopakumar and related engineering teams
- Justification: There is a clear product/platform functionality gap, explicitly discussed by Ramki and team, affecting client delivery and risk—the enhancement can be tracked as a Jira epics for automation and reconciliation improvements.

- VIII. Follow Up Questions/Suggestions

1. Request Quantitative KPI Reporting: For next meetings, prepare snapshots of numerical KPIs (processing turnaround times, dispute stats, error rates) alongside qualitative updates, addressing Ramki’s explicit expectation for measurable process health.
2. Presales/Whitepaper Integration: Begin collating and drafting differentiated features and operational wins as suggested, and share initial drafts with Ramki for feedback—ensure alignment before wider presales distribution.
3. Periodic Cross-team Reviews: Monitor effectiveness of the newly required handshake between product ops and platform support teams; schedule periodic check-ins to prevent “design-in-silos” situations.
4. Validate LOV/API Transition: Post-migration of reason codes/lists to Delta-managed APIs, schedule a walkthrough/demo to confirm broad adoption in all relevant workflows/UI and gather feedback.
5. Monitor UPI Adjustment File Automation Progress: Track progress and risks on standardization, especially new org codes/interfaces, and flag if any workflow gap/blocker materializes.

metadata.system_enhancements: GL coding, reason code management, automation items

title: 2024-11-25_Processing Services Check-in
